name: 0.6 M Na+ soda buffer mineral medium with thiocyanate (PMC5744179)
description: 0.6 M Na+ soda buffer mineral medium with thiocyanate — medium reported in PMC5744179, extracted for GrowthDB and mapped to BiGG.

provenance.citation: Medium composition from PMC5744179 (see GrowthDB record).
provenance.notes: Composition mapped to BiGG (mapper + salt dissociation); presence-based bounds; paper_amount retained. Added via GrowthDB curation.
provenance.verification: paper-verified (complete recipe; cited + supplementary sources chased)
provenance.verification_evidence: 0.6 M Na+ soda buffer at pH 9.8 (17.5 g · liter−1 Na2CO3, 13.9 g · liter−1 NaHCO3, 6.2 g · liter−1 NaCl, 1.0 g · liter−1 K2HPO4)
provenance.base_medium: 0.6 M Na+ soda buffer (pH 9.8) haloalkaliphilic mineral medium + Pfennig/Lippert (SL-4) trace elements; autotrophic (CO2/bicarbonate fixed via RuBisCO)

sources_chased.what: trace mineral solution cited as ref 38 (Pfennig & Lippert 1966, Arch Mikrobiol 55:245-256)
sources_chased.how: WebFetch mediadive.dsmz.de/rest/solution/20 (Trace element solution SL-4, the DSMZ 'Pfennig' trace element solution)

recuration.notes: Autotroph: carbon fixed from the carbonate/bicarbonate soda buffer (upregulated RuBisCO), no organic C source. Nitrogen for this (thiocyanate) medium comes from thiocyanate itself, so no NH4Cl is added. Trace solution = DSMZ SL-4 (the 'Pfennig' trace element solution, ref 38); Cu was boosted to 30 µg/L final because Cu is the TcDH cofactor. SL-4 contains NO vitamins, Se, or W, so those are genuinely absent. The auto-generated Calcium (EX_ca2_e) and Fe3+ (EX_fe3_e) entries have no support in the recipe (no Ca salt; only Fe2+ from FeSO4).
